claim_text: DISTS uses the same φ(l)_D as LPIPS, but with a distance function G inspired by the form of SSIM that assesses texture and structure similarity of the feature maps.

sub_claims.text: DISTS uses the same VGG feature representation φ(l)_D as LPIPS.

evidence.snippet: we also chose to base our model on the VGG16 CNN [14], pre-trained for object recognition [40] on the ImageNet database [41]... We modified the VGG architecture to achieve two additional desired properties... we replaced all max pooling layers in VGG with weighted ℓ2 pooling [42]

To ensure an injective mapping, we simply included the input image as an additional feature map (the "zeroth" layer of the network). The representation then consists of the input image x, concatenated with the convolution responses of five VGG layers (labelled conv1 2, conv2 2, conv3 3, conv4 3, and conv5 3)

we conducted ablation experiments to single out the individual contributions of key modifications of DISTS, in comparison to the most closely related alternative -LPIPS. We trained a series of intermediate models between LPIPS and DISTS... First, ℓ2 pooling is slightly better than max pooling... Second, incorporating the input image in the representation has little impact on the performance, but it ensures a unique minimum of DISTS

LPIPS, which is built on VGG but with no enforcement of the injective property, recovers most structures and details, but leaves some visible artifacts in the converged image (Fig. 2(j)). In contrast, DISTS successfully recovers the reference image from any initialization.

sub_claims.paper_value: VGG16 backbone with two modifications relative to LPIPS: (i) max-pooling replaced by ℓ2 pooling, (ii) input image x prepended as a 'zeroth' feature layer to make φ injective
sub_claims.nuance: Both methods extract VGG features from the same five conv stages (conv1_2..conv5_3), but ding2020 explicitly states DISTS modifies the VGG transformation in two ways relative to LPIPS (ℓ2 pooling instead of max pooling; input image included as a zeroth feature map for injectivity). Calling φ 'the same' is a defensible approximation but understates the architectural changes that ding2020 itself foregrounds in its ablation.

sub_claims.text: DISTS uses a distance function G inspired by the form of SSIM that assesses texture and structure similarity of the feature maps.

evidence.snippet: Inspired by the form of SSIM [3], we defined separate quality measurements for the texture (using the global means) and the structure (using the global correlations) of each pair of corresponding feature maps
we describe an image quality method that combines correlations of these spatial averages ("texture similarity") with correlations of the feature maps ("structure similarity")
show that the resulting Deep Image Structure and Texture Similarity (DISTS) index can be transformed into a proper metric in the mathematical sense
the global SSIM-like distance outperforms the Euclidean distance, especially in measuring similarity of visual textures and invariance to geometric transformations

sub_claims.nuance: Ding2020 explicitly states the DISTS distance is 'inspired by the form of SSIM' and is decomposed into texture (global means) and structure (global correlations) similarity over feature maps -- a verbatim match to the manuscript's wording.

remediation.suggested_edit: Soften 'uses the same φ(l)_D as LPIPS' to acknowledge ding2020's two stated modifications. Suggested rewording: 'DISTS uses a VGG feature representation closely related to LPIPS’s (with ℓ2-pooling in place of max-pooling and the input image included as a zeroth layer to ensure injectivity), combined with a distance function G inspired by the form of SSIM that assesses texture and structure similarity of the feature maps.'

phrasings_tried: DISTS uses the same VGG feature extractor / φ as LPIPS
DISTS modifies the VGG backbone relative to LPIPS (ℓ2 pooling, zeroth-layer input image for injectivity)
DISTS’s distance function G is inspired by SSIM and combines texture and structure similarity over feature maps

section_checklist.section: Connections to Other Full-Reference IQA Methods
Applications to Texture Classification and Retrieval

attestation.closest_adjacent: Ablation Study, line 1: 'we conducted ablation experiments to single out the individual contributions of key modifications of DISTS, in comparison to the most closely related alternative -LPIPS. We trained a series of intermediate models between LPIPS and DISTS' -- this is the closest passage to a 'same φ as LPIPS' framing, but it is positioned as an inventory of differences (ℓ2 pooling, injective zeroth layer, SSIM-like distance, E2 training term) rather than an identity claim.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: ding2020 is the primary source for DISTS, so no indirect attribution issue. The φ used by LPIPS is itself attributed to Zhang et al. [7], cited by ding2020.
attestation.out_of_context_check: ding2020 explicitly contrasts its modified VGG transformation with LPIPS’s, both narratively (Initial Transformation, Fig. 2 reference-recovery comparison) and quantitatively (Ablation Study Table 6). The manuscript’s 'same φ(l)_D as LPIPS' framing therefore lightly misrepresents how ding2020 positions its own feature extractor; the SSIM-inspired distance-function half of the claim matches ding2020 exactly.
